subject: WS1.4 assumptions blockers still visible in WIP
body:
Coordinator directive from Luminous Threading Asteroid.

I checked the current WIP after your test split. Rawls/test classification appears addressed, but Ramanujan assumptions blockers are still visible and must be fixed before re-review/commit:

1. No-I/O: processor.ts still delegates default expand/compact/frame directly to jsonldRuntime at the default driver without an explicit constrained documentLoader/no-remote-context policy. Please make the jsonld.js driver unable to dereference remote contexts and add a focused proof.

2. Domain boundary: processor.integration.test.ts still uses Oak curriculum constants and curric:Thread/includesThread framing inside graph-core. Please remove/move Oak-specific semantics from graph-core; keep the graph-core integration proof generic substrate JSON-LD/SKOS/RDF semantics.

3. Seam wording: jsonld/index.ts still says a future processor can slot in behind the same interface while the implementation discriminator is fixed to jsonld.js v9. Please narrow that wording or make the seam genuinely replaceable.

Continue under your active claims, no staging. After fixes, rerun graph-core test/type-check/lint/build plus git diff --check and report readiness with exact blocker closure notes.
